title: podman push: 500 Internal Server Error with Harbor
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Unable to push images with podman to Harbor registry created through Bitnami helm chart. Every push is met with `500 Internal Service Error`. Issue does not occur when using Docker to push.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Deploy Bitnami Harbor Chart

2. Setup Ingress accordingly

3. Push image to Harbor registry

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Writing manifest to image destination
Copying blob 99b5261d397c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2a8e1e567b0b skipped: already exists
DEBU[0000] ... already exists
Copying blob 99b5261d397c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 99b5261d397c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2a8e1e567b0b skipped: already exists
Copying blob 99b5261d397c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2a8e1e567b0b skipped: already exists
Copying blob d05f202848f3 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0c6f7f5f6419 skipped: already exists
Copying blob e4102d5e6f43 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
DEBU[0000] exporting opaque data as blob "sha256:3a8ef3de58ba30e71b027b89ed740a407a2bf5ea10e20395d7762cbccd990456"
DEBU[0000] No compression detected
DEBU[0000] Using original blob without modification
DEBU[0000] Checking /v2/library/sonarr/blobs/sha256:3a8ef3de58ba30e71b027b89ed740a407a2bf5ea10e20395d7762cbccd990456
DEBU[0000] HEAD https://harbor.svc.example.com/v2/library/testapp/blobs/sha256:3a8ef3de58ba30e71b027b89ed740a407a2bf5ea10e20395d7762cbccd990456
DEBU[0000] ... already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
DEBU[0000] PUT https://harbor.svc.example.com/v2/library/testapp/manifests/latest
DEBU[0000] Writing manifest using preferred type application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json failed: Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to harbor.svc.example.com/library/testapp: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error
ERRO[0000] Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to harbor.svc.example.com/library/testapp: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error

```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Attempted with podman 1.7.0 from Fedora 30 rpm and podman 1.8.2 built from source. Same error.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.7.0-3.fc30.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Virtual machine on ESXi

comments.body:
Are you building your own images, if so then maybe the Harbor registry does not support OCI images?

You can build your images with the --format docker and see if the images are accepted then.

podman build --format docker ...

```
man podman build
...
       --format

       Control  the  format  for  the  built image's manifest and configuration data.  Recognized formats
       include oci (OCI image-spec v1.0, the default) and docker (version 2, using schema  format  2  for
       the manifest).

       Note: You can also override the default format by setting the BUILDAH_FORMAT environment variable.
       export BUILDAH_FORMAT=docker
```
If this is true,could you contact the Harbor people and ask them to support the standard format?

That worked, I'll need to prune the old images as rebuilding them does not appear to generate the new manifest (easy fix). I will open an issue with `goharbor` and cite this one as the cause is definitely related to the OCI manifest. Thanks!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
